---
title: "Random Forest Classification"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(ranger)
library(caret)
library(yardstick)
library(ggnewscale)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(mltools)

incident_data <- read.csv(here("output", "clustered_incident_data.csv"))

subtype_levels <- c("Theft", "Vehicle Theft", "Vandalism",
                    "Burglary", "Shoplifting",
                    "Assault", "Robbery", "Sexual Offenses", "Other Violent")

##log-transform skewed continuous predictors; factorize outcome and cluster
incident_data <- incident_data |>
  mutate(
    log_median_income = log(median_income),
    log_total_pop     = log1p(total_pop),
    outcome           = as.factor(outcome),
    outcome_subtype = factor(outcome_subtype, levels = subtype_levels),
    cluster           = factor(cluster)
  )

##ibm colorblind palette
ibm_blue    <- "#648FFF"
ibm_purple  <- "#785EF0"
ibm_magenta <- "#DC267F"
ibm_orange  <- "#FE6100"
ibm_gold    <- "#FFB000"

##styling
theme_ibm <- function() {
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#E8EEFF", color = NA),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90")
  )
}

kable_style <- function(kbl) {
  kbl |>
    kableExtra::kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position"),
      stripe_color  = "#f5f5f5",
      font_size     = 10
    )
}

extract_f1 <- function(cm_obj) {
  bs     <- cm_obj$byClass
  f1_vec <- bs[, "F1"]
  list(per_class = f1_vec,
       macro     = mean(f1_vec, na.rm = TRUE))
}

compute_mcc <- function(preds, actuals) {
  mltools::mcc(preds = as.factor(preds), actuals = as.factor(actuals))
}
```

\newpage

# Crime Type Classification

```{r predictors}
##full predictor pool: socioeconomic, demographic, and cluster membership
predictor_pool <- c(
  "log_median_income",
  "gini",
  "poverty_rate",
  "no_vehicle_rate",
  "housing_burden_rate",
  "vacancy_rate",
  "renter_rate",
  "single_parent_rate",
  "lang_isolation_rate",
  "internet_rate",
  "log_total_pop",
  "median_age",
  "pct_black",
  "pct_asian",
  "edu_rate_no_hsdip",
  "disability_rate",
  "cluster"
)

##drop rows with missing values on any predictor
incident_data <- incident_data |>
  filter(if_all(all_of(predictor_pool), ~ !is.na(.)))
```

```{r split}
##80/20 train-test split
set.seed(490)

train_idx <- sample(
  seq_len(nrow(incident_data)),
  size = floor(0.80 * nrow(incident_data))
)

train <- incident_data[train_idx, ]
test  <- incident_data[-train_idx, ]
```

## Hyperparameter Tuning

```{r mtry-tune, cache=TRUE}
rf_formula <- as.formula(
  paste("outcome ~", paste(predictor_pool, collapse = " + "))
)

##mtry fixed at 6 (sqrt(17) default) based on prior run showing flat F1 across all mtry
##tuning budget focused on min.node.size; 100 trees during CV, 300 for final fit
min_node_grid <- c(1, 5, 10, 20, 30)

set.seed(490)
folds <- createFolds(train$outcome, k = 5, list = TRUE, returnTrain = TRUE)

##5-fold CV over min.node.size, selecting on mean F1
mtry_cv <- map_dfr(min_node_grid, function(mn) {
  fold_f1 <- sapply(seq_along(folds), function(i) {
    tr  <- train[folds[[i]], ]
    val <- train[-folds[[i]], ]

    ##upsample minority class within fold to correct imbalance
    tr_up <- upSample(x = tr[, predictor_pool],
                      y = tr$outcome,
                      yname = "outcome")

    fit <- ranger(
      rf_formula,
      data          = tr_up,
      num.trees     = 100,
      mtry          = 6,
      min.node.size = mn,
      num.threads   = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
      verbose       = FALSE,
      seed          = 490
    )

    preds <- predict(fit, data = val)$predictions
    y_val <- factor(val$outcome, levels = c("0", "1"))
    cm_t  <- confusionMatrix(preds, y_val, positive = "1")
    prec  <- cm_t$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
    rec   <- cm_t$byClass["Sensitivity"]
    ifelse(is.na(prec) | is.na(rec) | (prec + rec) == 0,
           0, 2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec))
  })

  tibble(min_node = mn, mean_f1 = mean(fold_f1), sd_f1 = sd(fold_f1))
})

best_min_node <- mtry_cv$min_node[which.max(mtry_cv$mean_f1)]
best_mtry     <- 6
```

```{r mtry-cv-table}
##cv results across min.node.size; mtry fixed at 6
mtry_cv |>
  mutate(
    mean_f1  = round(mean_f1, 4),
    sd_f1    = round(sd_f1,   4),
    Selected = if_else(min_node == best_min_node, "Yes", "")
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(
    caption   = "5-Fold CV F1 by min.node.size (mtry fixed at 6)",
    col.names = c("min.node.size", "Mean CV F1", "SD", "Selected"),
    align     = c("r", "r", "r", "c")
  ) |>
  kable_style()
```

```{r mtry-plot, fig.width=6, fig.height=4, out.width="80%", fig.align="center"}
##mtry fixed at 6; plot min.node.size on x-axis
ggplot(mtry_cv, aes(x = min_node, y = mean_f1)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_f1 - sd_f1,
                  ymax = mean_f1 + sd_f1),
              fill = ibm_blue, alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_line(color = ibm_blue, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = ibm_blue) +
  geom_point(
    data   = filter(mtry_cv, min_node == best_min_node),
    size   = 4,
    shape  = 21,
    fill   = ibm_orange,
    color  = "white",
    stroke = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = min_node_grid) +
  labs(
    title    = "5-Fold CV F1 by min.node.size (mtry fixed at 6)",
    subtitle = paste0("Optimal min.node.size = ", best_min_node,
                      " (highlighted); shaded band = \u00b11 SD"),
    x        = "min.node.size",
    y        = "Mean CV F1"
  ) +
  theme_ibm()
```

\newpage

## Threshold Tuning

```{r threshold-tune, cache=TRUE}
##sweep classification thresholds using the same 5 folds
##probability = TRUE required to get calibrated probabilities for threshold sweep
thresholds <- seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.01)
fold_f1    <- matrix(NA, nrow = 5, ncol = length(thresholds))

for (i in seq_along(folds)) {
  tr  <- train[folds[[i]], ]
  val <- train[-folds[[i]], ]

  ##upsample minority class within fold
  tr_up <- upSample(x = tr[, predictor_pool],
                    y = tr$outcome,
                    yname = "outcome")

  fit <- ranger(
    rf_formula,
    data          = tr_up,
    num.trees     = 100,
    mtry          = best_mtry,
    min.node.size = best_min_node,
    probability   = TRUE,
    num.threads   = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
    verbose       = FALSE,
    seed          = 490
  )

  probs <- predict(fit, data = val)$predictions[, "1"]
  y_val <- factor(val$outcome, levels = c("0", "1"))

  for (j in seq_along(thresholds)) {
    preds <- factor(ifelse(probs > thresholds[j], 1, 0),
                    levels = levels(y_val))
    cm_t  <- confusionMatrix(preds, y_val, positive = "1")
    prec  <- cm_t$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
    rec   <- cm_t$byClass["Sensitivity"]
    fold_f1[i, j] <- ifelse(
      is.na(prec) | is.na(rec) | (prec + rec) == 0,
      0, 2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec)
    )
  }
}

mean_f1      <- colMeans(fold_f1, na.rm = TRUE)
best_thr     <- thresholds[which.max(mean_f1)]
best_thr_idx <- which.max(mean_f1)

##per-fold F1 at selected threshold
tibble(
  Fold = as.character(1:5),
  F1   = round(fold_f1[, best_thr_idx], 4)
) |>
  bind_rows(tibble(Fold = "Mean",
                   F1   = round(mean(fold_f1[, best_thr_idx]), 4))) |>
  knitr::kable(
    caption   = paste0("5-Fold CV F1 at Selected Threshold (", best_thr, ")"),
    col.names = c("Fold", "F1")
  ) |>
  kable_style()
```

```{r threshold-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=4, out.width="100%"}
tibble(Threshold = thresholds, MeanF1 = mean_f1) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Threshold, y = MeanF1)) +
  geom_line(color = ibm_blue, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = best_thr,
             linetype = "dashed", color = ibm_orange, linewidth = 0.6) +
  annotate("text", x = best_thr, y = min(mean_f1),
           label = paste0("Threshold = ", best_thr),
           hjust = -0.1, size = 2.8, color = "gray30") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Mean CV F1 Across Thresholds",
    subtitle = paste0("mtry = ", best_mtry, ", min.node.size = ", best_min_node,
                      "; orange dashed line marks CV-selected threshold"),
    x        = "Classification Threshold",
    y        = "Mean CV F1"
  ) +
  theme_ibm()
```

```{r train, cache=TRUE}
##upsample full training set before final fit
train_up <- upSample(x = train[, predictor_pool],
                     y = train$outcome,
                     yname = "outcome")

##refit with best params and probability = TRUE for threshold application
rf_model <- ranger(
  rf_formula,
  data          = train_up,
  num.trees     = 300,
  mtry          = best_mtry,
  min.node.size = best_min_node,
  importance    = "permutation",
  probability   = TRUE,
  num.threads   = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
  verbose       = FALSE,
  seed          = 490
)
```

\newpage

## Model Performance

```{r confusion-matrix, fig.height=3.8, fig.width=4.5, fig.align="center"}
pred_prob   <- predict(rf_model, data = test)$predictions[, "1"]
predictions <- factor(ifelse(pred_prob > best_thr, 1, 0),
                      levels = levels(test$outcome))

cm_rf <- confusionMatrix(predictions, test$outcome, positive = "1")

cm_data <- as.data.frame(cm_rf$table) |>
  rename(Predicted = Prediction, Actual = Reference) |>
  mutate(
    Actual    = factor(Actual,    levels = c("1", "0")),
    Predicted = factor(Predicted, levels = c("0", "1")),
    class_label = case_when(
      Predicted == "0" & Actual == "0" ~ "True Negative",
      Predicted == "1" & Actual == "1" ~ "True Positive",
      Predicted == "1" & Actual == "0" ~ "False Positive",
      Predicted == "0" & Actual == "1" ~ "False Negative"
    ),
    correct = Predicted == Actual,
    pct     = round(Freq / sum(Freq) * 100, 1),
    label   = paste0(formatC(Freq, format = "d", big.mark = ","),
                     "\n(", pct, "%)")
  )

ggplot(cm_data, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_data, correct),
            aes(fill = Freq), color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F5E6FF", high = ibm_magenta,
                      guide = "none") +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_data, !correct),
            aes(fill = Freq), color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#E8F0FF", high = ibm_blue,
                      guide = "none") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 3.8, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(label = class_label),
            vjust = 4.5, size = 2.6, color = "gray20") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("1" = "Violent", "0" = "Property")) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = c("1" = "Violent", "0" = "Property")) +
  labs(title    = "Confusion Matrix: Crime Type",
       subtitle = paste0("mtry = ", best_mtry, ", min.node.size = ", best_min_node,
                         ", threshold = ", best_thr),
       x = "Actual Class", y = "Predicted Class") +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), axis.ticks = element_blank())
```

```{r performance}
##build results tibble for yardstick metrics
results <- tibble(
  truth    = test$outcome,
  estimate = predictions
)

##f1 and mcc via yardstick for consistency with other models
f1  <- f_meas(results, truth = truth, estimate = estimate, event_level = "second")$.estimate
mcc <- yardstick::mcc(results, truth = truth, estimate = estimate)$.estimate

tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "F1 Score",
    "Matthews Correlation Coefficient",
    "Accuracy",
    "Sensitivity / Recall",
    "Specificity",
    "Precision",
    "Balanced Accuracy"
  ),
  Value = round(c(
    f1,
    mcc,
    cm_rf$overall["Accuracy"],
    cm_rf$byClass["Sensitivity"],
    cm_rf$byClass["Specificity"],
    cm_rf$byClass["Pos Pred Value"],
    cm_rf$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]
  ), 4)
) |>
  knitr::kable(
    caption = "Random Forest Classification Performance",
    align   = c("l", "r")
  ) |>
  kable_style()
```

\newpage

## Variable Importance

```{r importance, fig.width=9, fig.height=6, out.width="100%"}
importance_df <- data.frame(
  Variable   = names(rf_model$variable.importance),
  Importance = rf_model$variable.importance
) |>
  arrange(desc(Importance)) |>
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(
      Variable,
      log_median_income   = "Median Income (log)",
      gini                = "Gini Coefficient",
      poverty_rate        = "Poverty Rate",
      no_vehicle_rate     = "No Vehicle Rate",
      housing_burden_rate = "Housing Burden Rate",
      vacancy_rate        = "Vacancy Rate",
      renter_rate         = "Renter Rate",
      single_parent_rate  = "Single-Parent Rate",
      lang_isolation_rate = "Language Isolation Rate",
      internet_rate       = "Internet Access Rate",
      log_total_pop       = "Total Population (log)",
      median_age          = "Median Age",
      pct_black           = "% Black",
      pct_asian           = "% Asian",
      edu_rate_no_hsdip   = "No HS Diploma Rate",
      disability_rate     = "Disability Rate",
      cluster             = "Neighborhood Cluster"
    )
  )

ggplot(
  importance_df,
  aes(
    x = reorder(Variable, Importance),
    y = Importance
  )
) +
  geom_col(fill = ibm_blue) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest Variable Importance",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean Decrease in Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_ibm()
```

\newpage

# Crime Subtype Classification

## Hyperparameter Tuning

```{r subtype-mtry-tune, cache=TRUE}
subtype_formula <- as.formula(
  paste("outcome_subtype ~", paste(predictor_pool, collapse = " + "))
)

##mtry fixed at 10 based on prior run showing flat macro F1 across all mtry
##tuning budget focused on min.node.size; 100 trees during CV, 300 for final fit
set.seed(490)
subtype_folds <- createFolds(train$outcome_subtype, k = 5,
                             list = TRUE, returnTrain = TRUE)

subtype_mtry_cv <- map_dfr(min_node_grid, function(mn) {
  fold_f1 <- sapply(seq_along(subtype_folds), function(i) {
    tr  <- train[subtype_folds[[i]], ]
    val <- train[-subtype_folds[[i]], ]

    ##upsample all minority subtypes within fold
    tr_up <- upSample(x = tr[, predictor_pool],
                      y = tr$outcome_subtype,
                      yname = "outcome_subtype")

    fit <- ranger(
      subtype_formula,
      data          = tr_up,
      num.trees     = 100,
      mtry          = 10,
      min.node.size = mn,
      num.threads   = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
      verbose       = FALSE,
      seed          = 490
    )

    preds <- predict(fit, data = val)$predictions
    y_val <- factor(val$outcome_subtype, levels = subtype_levels)
    cm_t  <- confusionMatrix(preds, y_val)
    mean(cm_t$byClass[, "F1"], na.rm = TRUE)
  })

  tibble(min_node = mn, mean_f1 = mean(fold_f1), sd_f1 = sd(fold_f1))
})

best_min_node_subtype <- subtype_mtry_cv$min_node[which.max(subtype_mtry_cv$mean_f1)]
best_mtry_subtype     <- 10
```

```{r subtype-mtry-table}
subtype_mtry_cv |>
  mutate(
    mean_f1  = round(mean_f1, 4),
    sd_f1    = round(sd_f1,   4),
    Selected = if_else(min_node == best_min_node_subtype, "Yes", "")
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(
    caption   = "5-Fold CV Macro F1 by min.node.size (mtry fixed at 10)",
    col.names = c("min.node.size", "Mean CV Macro F1", "SD", "Selected"),
    align     = c("r", "r", "r", "c")
  ) |>
  kable_style()
```

```{r subtype-mtry-plot, fig.width=6, fig.height=4, out.width="80%", fig.align="center"}
##mtry fixed at 10; plot min.node.size on x-axis
ggplot(subtype_mtry_cv, aes(x = min_node, y = mean_f1)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_f1 - sd_f1,
                  ymax = mean_f1 + sd_f1),
              fill = ibm_blue, alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_line(color = ibm_blue, linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = ibm_blue) +
  geom_point(
    data   = filter(subtype_mtry_cv, min_node == best_min_node_subtype),
    size   = 4,
    shape  = 21,
    fill   = ibm_orange,
    color  = "white",
    stroke = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = min_node_grid) +
  labs(
    title    = "5-Fold CV Macro F1 by min.node.size (Crime Subtype)",
    subtitle = paste0("mtry fixed at 10; optimal min.node.size = ",
                      best_min_node_subtype,
                      " (highlighted); shaded band = \u00b11 SD"),
    x        = "min.node.size",
    y        = "Mean CV Macro F1"
  ) +
  theme_ibm()
```

## Final Model

```{r subtype-train, cache=TRUE}
##upsample full training set before final subtype fit
train_up_sub <- upSample(x = train[, predictor_pool],
                         y = train$outcome_subtype,
                         yname = "outcome_subtype")

rf_subtype <- ranger(
  subtype_formula,
  data          = train_up_sub,
  num.trees     = 300,
  mtry          = best_mtry_subtype,
  min.node.size = best_min_node_subtype,
  importance    = "permutation",
  num.threads   = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
  seed          = 490
)
```

## Performance

```{r subtype-cm, fig.height=5.5, fig.width=7, fig.align="center"}
test_outcome_subtype <- factor(test$outcome_subtype, levels = subtype_levels)
pred_subtype         <- predict(rf_subtype, data = test)$predictions

cm_subtype  <- confusionMatrix(pred_subtype, test_outcome_subtype)
f1_subtype  <- extract_f1(cm_subtype)
mcc_subtype <- compute_mcc(pred_subtype, test_outcome_subtype)

as.data.frame(cm_subtype$table) |>
  rename(Predicted = Prediction, Actual = Reference) |>
  mutate(
    Predicted    = factor(Predicted, levels = rev(subtype_levels)),
    Actual       = factor(Actual,    levels = subtype_levels),
    fill_cat     = case_when(
      Freq == 0           ~ "empty",
      Predicted == Actual ~ "correct",
      TRUE                ~ "incorrect"
    ),
    alpha_scaled = case_when(
      Freq == 0 ~ 0.35,
      TRUE      ~ 0.15 + 0.85 * (Freq / max(Freq))
    )
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = fill_cat, alpha = alpha_scaled),
            color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Freq > 0,
                               formatC(Freq, format = "d", big.mark = ","), "")),
            size = 2.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("correct"   = ibm_magenta,
                               "incorrect" = ibm_blue,
                               "empty"     = "gray70"),
                    labels  = c("correct"   = "True",
                                "incorrect" = "False",
                                "empty"     = "No predictions"),
                    name    = NULL) +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.15, 1), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 35)) +
  labs(title    = "Confusion Matrix: Crime Subtype",
       subtitle = paste0("mtry = ", best_mtry_subtype,
                         ", min.node.size = ", best_min_node_subtype),
       x = "Actual Subtype", y = "Predicted Subtype") +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(panel.grid      = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks      = element_blank(),
        axis.text       = element_text(size = 7),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r subtype-per-class-f1}
tibble(
  Subtype = subtype_levels,
  Type    = c(rep("Property", 5), rep("Violent", 4)),
  F1      = round(f1_subtype$per_class[paste0("Class: ", subtype_levels)], 3)
) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Per-Subtype F1 Score",
               col.names = c("Subtype", "Type", "F1")) |>
  kable_style() |>
  kableExtra::pack_rows("Property", 1, 5, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8e8e8") |>
  kableExtra::pack_rows("Violent",  6, 9, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8e8e8")
```

```{r subtype-f1-plot, fig.height=4, fig.width=6.5, fig.align="center"}
f1_type <- f_meas(
  tibble(truth    = test$outcome,
         estimate = predictions),
  truth = truth, estimate = estimate, event_level = "second"
)$.estimate

tibble(
  Subtype = factor(subtype_levels, levels = rev(subtype_levels)),
  Type    = c(rep("Property", 5), rep("Violent", 4)),
  F1      = f1_subtype$per_class[paste0("Class: ", subtype_levels)]
) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Subtype, y = F1, fill = Type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = f1_subtype$macro,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = f1_type,
             linetype = "dashed", color = ibm_blue, linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.6, y = f1_subtype$macro,
           label = paste0(" Macro F1 = ", round(f1_subtype$macro, 3)),
           vjust = -0.4, hjust = 0, size = 2.8, color = "gray40") +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = f1_type,
         label = paste0("Crime Type F1 = ", round(f1_type, 3)),
         vjust = -0.4, hjust = 1.1, size = 2.8, color = ibm_blue) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Property" = ibm_purple, "Violent" = ibm_magenta)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, max(f1_subtype$per_class, f1_type, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.18)
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title    = "Per-Subtype F1 Score",
       subtitle = "Gray dashed = subtype macro F1; blue dashed = crime type F1",
       x = NULL, y = "F1 Score", fill = NULL) +
  theme_ibm() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r subtype-overall}
tibble(
  Metric = c("Macro F1", "MCC", "Weighted F1", "Overall Accuracy"),
  Value  = round(c(
    f1_subtype$macro,
    mcc_subtype,
    sum(f1_subtype$per_class *
          (table(test_outcome_subtype) / length(test_outcome_subtype)),
        na.rm = TRUE),
    cm_subtype$overall["Accuracy"]
  ), 4)
) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Overall Crime Subtype Performance (Test Set)",
               col.names = c("Metric", "Value")) |>
  kable_style()
```

```{r subtype-importance, fig.width=9, fig.height=6, out.width="100%"}
importance_df_sub <- data.frame(
  Variable   = names(rf_subtype$variable.importance),
  Importance = rf_subtype$variable.importance
) |>
  arrange(desc(Importance)) |>
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(
      Variable,
      log_median_income   = "Median Income (log)",
      gini                = "Gini Coefficient",
      poverty_rate        = "Poverty Rate",
      no_vehicle_rate     = "No Vehicle Rate",
      housing_burden_rate = "Housing Burden Rate",
      vacancy_rate        = "Vacancy Rate",
      renter_rate         = "Renter Rate",
      single_parent_rate  = "Single-Parent Rate",
      lang_isolation_rate = "Language Isolation Rate",
      internet_rate       = "Internet Access Rate",
      log_total_pop       = "Total Population (log)",
      median_age          = "Median Age",
      pct_black           = "% Black",
      pct_asian           = "% Asian",
      edu_rate_no_hsdip   = "No HS Diploma Rate",
      disability_rate     = "Disability Rate",
      cluster             = "Neighborhood Cluster"
    )
  )

ggplot(
  importance_df_sub,
  aes(
    x = reorder(Variable, Importance),
    y = Importance
  )
) +
  geom_col(fill = ibm_blue) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title    = "Random Forest Variable Importance: Crime Subtype",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Mean Decrease in Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_ibm()
```
